justification: All 6 ACs implemented and tested. AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED via STATUS_MAP. AC4 uses logger.warn (not logger.error) and returns unknown. AC5 uses (total_amount / 100).toFixed(2) for proper floating-point conversion. AC6 handles both null and undefined customer_name. 16 tests cover all 6 ACs with explicit names, AC3 tested with both inputs, AC4 verifies logger.warn called. acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. Logger is injected (not global). No external dependencies.

observations.text: AC3 explicitly maps both FAILED and DECLINED to failed in STATUS_MAP
AC4 correctly uses logger.warn with message containing original status and order id
AC5 uses parseFloat((raw.total_amount / 100).toFixed(2)) — correct floating-point handling
AC6 guards on != null which covers both null and undefined
16 tests organized by AC with descriptive names, including edge cases like empty string, case sensitivity, and zero amount
No external dependencies, logger injected via parameter
